| MCP:YQnf8p | # SpringBoot Java Template | MCP:YQnf8p | | MCP:YQnf8p | [![Twitter](https://img.shields.io/twitter/url?style=social&url=https%3A%2F%2Ftwitter.com%2Fgeminikims)](https://twitter.com/geminikims) | MCP:YQnf8p | [![Youtube](https://img.shields.io/youtube/channel/views/UCDh8zEDofOcrOMAOnSVL9Tg?label=Youtube&style=social)](https://www.youtube.com/@geminikims) | MCP:YQnf8p | [![CI](https://github.com/team-dodn/spring-boot-java-template/actions/workflows/ci.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/team-dodn/spring-boot-java-template/actions/workflows/ci.yml) | MCP:YQnf8p | [![License](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-Apache%202.0-green.svg)](https://opensource.org/licenses/Apache-2.0) | MCP:YQnf8p | | MCP:YQnf8p | This is not the best structure. This is a good basic structure to use early in the project when productivity is important. | MCP:YQnf8p | | MCP:YQnf8p | Remember, as your software grows, your structure must grow too. | MCP:YQnf8p | | MCP:YQnf8p | # **Modules** | MCP:YQnf8p | | MCP:YQnf8p | ## Core | MCP:YQnf8p | | MCP:YQnf8p | > Only describe the core:core-api module and ignore the rest of the module groups because they are implementation noise. | MCP:YQnf8p | | MCP:YQnf8p | Each submodule of this module is responsible for one domain service. | MCP:YQnf8p | | MCP:YQnf8p | This must make the modular structure grow with the growth of the service. | MCP:YQnf8p | | MCP:YQnf8p | ### core:core-api | MCP:YQnf8p | It is the only executable module in the project. It is structured to have domains to maximize initial development productivity. | MCP:YQnf8p | | MCP:YQnf8p | It is also responsible for providing APIs and setting up frameworks for services. | MCP:YQnf8p | | MCP:YQnf8p | ### core:core-enum | MCP:YQnf8p | | MCP:YQnf8p | This module contains enums that are used by `core-api` and must be delivered to external modules. | MCP:YQnf8p | | MCP:YQnf8p |
| MCP:YQnf8p | | MCP:YQnf8p | ## Clients | MCP:YQnf8p | Submodules of this module are responsible for integrating with external systems. | MCP:YQnf8p | | MCP:YQnf8p | ### clients:clients-example | MCP:YQnf8p | This module shows an example of HTTP communication with `Spring-Cloud-Open-Feign`. | MCP:YQnf8p | | MCP:YQnf8p |
| MCP:YQnf8p | | MCP:YQnf8p | ## Storage | MCP:YQnf8p | Submodules of this module are responsible for integrating with the various storages. | MCP:YQnf8p | | MCP:YQnf8p | ### storage:db-core | MCP:YQnf8p | This module shows an example of connecting to `MySql` using `Spring-Data-JPA`. | MCP:YQnf8p | | MCP:YQnf8p |
| MCP:YQnf8p | | MCP:YQnf8p | ## Support | MCP:YQnf8p | Submodules of this module are responsible for additional support. | MCP:YQnf8p | | MCP:YQnf8p | ### support:logging | MCP:YQnf8p | This module supports logging of service and has a dependency added for distributed tracing support. | MCP:YQnf8p | | MCP:YQnf8p | It also includes dependencies to support `Sentry`. | MCP:YQnf8p | | MCP:YQnf8p | ### support:monitoring | MCP:YQnf8p | This module supports monitoring of services. | MCP:YQnf8p | | MCP:YQnf8p |
| MCP:YQnf8p | | MCP:YQnf8p | ## Tests | MCP:YQnf8p | Submodules of this module are responsible for the convenience of writing test codes. | MCP:YQnf8p | | MCP:YQnf8p | ### tests:api-docs | MCP:YQnf8p | This module is for writing spring-rest-docs conveniently. | MCP:YQnf8p | | MCP:YQnf8p |
| MCP:YQnf8p | | MCP:YQnf8p | # Dependency Management | MCP:YQnf8p | All dependency versioning is done through `gradle.properties` file. | MCP:YQnf8p | | MCP:YQnf8p | If you want to add a new dependency, put the version in `gradle.properties` and load it in `build.gradle`. | MCP:YQnf8p | | MCP:YQnf8p |
| MCP:YQnf8p | | MCP:YQnf8p | # Runtime Profiles | MCP:YQnf8p | | MCP:YQnf8p | ## local | MCP:YQnf8p | This profile aims to configure an environment that can be developed even if the network is disconnected. | MCP:YQnf8p | | MCP:YQnf8p | ## local-dev | MCP:YQnf8p | This profile aims configurations that allow me to connect to the DEV environment from my local machine. | MCP:YQnf8p | | MCP:YQnf8p | ## dev | MCP:YQnf8p | This profile exists for deploying Development environments. | MCP:YQnf8p | | MCP:YQnf8p | ## staging | MCP:YQnf8p | This profile exists for deploying Staging environments. | MCP:YQnf8p | | MCP:YQnf8p | ## live | MCP:YQnf8p | This profile exists for deploying Live environments. | MCP:YQnf8p | | MCP:YQnf8p |
| MCP:YQnf8p | | MCP:YQnf8p | # Test Tasks & Tags | MCP:YQnf8p | | MCP:YQnf8p | ## test | MCP:YQnf8p | This is a collection of test-tasks that we want to run on `CI`. | MCP:YQnf8p | | MCP:YQnf8p | If you want to change the settings, modify the `build.gradle` file. | MCP:YQnf8p | | MCP:YQnf8p | ## unitTest | MCP:YQnf8p | This is a group of tests that typically have no dependencies, are fast to run, and test a single feature. | MCP:YQnf8p | | MCP:YQnf8p | ## contextTest | MCP:YQnf8p | This is a task that runs with SpringContext and has integration tests. | MCP:YQnf8p | | MCP:YQnf8p | ## restDocsTest | MCP:YQnf8p | This is a task to create asciidoc based on spring-rest-docs. | MCP:YQnf8p | | MCP:YQnf8p | ## developTest | MCP:YQnf8p | This is a task of tests that should not be run in `CI`. | MCP:YQnf8p | | MCP:YQnf8p | This is a good tag to use if you're not good at writing tests. | MCP:YQnf8p | | MCP:YQnf8p |
| MCP:YQnf8p | | MCP:YQnf8p | # Recommended Preferences | MCP:YQnf8p | | MCP:YQnf8p | ## Git Hook | MCP:YQnf8p | This setting makes run `lint` on every commit. | MCP:YQnf8p | | MCP:YQnf8p | ``` | MCP:YQnf8p | $ git config core.hookspath .githooks | MCP:YQnf8p | ``` | MCP:YQnf8p | | MCP:YQnf8p | ## IntelliJ IDEA | MCP:YQnf8p | This setting makes it easier to run the `test code` out of the box. | MCP:YQnf8p | | MCP:YQnf8p | ``` | MCP:YQnf8p | // Gradle Build and run with IntelliJ IDEA | MCP:YQnf8p | Build, Execution, Deployment > Build Tools > Gradle > Run tests using > IntelliJ IDEA | MCP:YQnf8p | ``` | MCP:YQnf8p | | MCP:YQnf8p | If you want to apply lint settings to the format of IDEA, please refer to the guide below. | MCP:YQnf8p | | MCP:YQnf8p | [Spring Java Format IntelliJ IDEA](https://github.com/spring-io/spring-javaformat#intellij-idea) | MCP:YQnf8p | | MCP:YQnf8p | --- | MCP:YQnf8p | | MCP:YQnf8p | # Supported By | MCP:YQnf8p |

| MCP:YQnf8p | pluginManagement {
| MCP:YQnf8p |     plugins {
| MCP:YQnf8p |         id 'org.springframework.boot' version "${springBootVersion}"
| MCP:YQnf8p |         id 'io.spring.dependency-management' version "${springDependencyManagementVersion}"
| MCP:YQnf8p |         id 'org.asciidoctor.jvm.convert' version "${asciidoctorConvertVersion}"
| MCP:YQnf8p |         id 'io.spring.javaformat' version "${springJavaFormatVersion}"
| MCP:YQnf8p |     }
| MCP:YQnf8p | }
| MCP:YQnf8p | 
| MCP:YQnf8p | rootProject.name = 'spring-boot-java-template'
| MCP:YQnf8p | 
| MCP:YQnf8p | include 'core:core-enum'
| MCP:YQnf8p | include 'core:core-api'
| MCP:YQnf8p | include 'storage:db-core'
| MCP:YQnf8p | include 'tests:api-docs'
| MCP:YQnf8p | include 'support:logging'
| MCP:YQnf8p | include 'support:monitoring'
| MCP:YQnf8p | include 'clients:client-example'

| MCP:YQnf8p | bootJar.enabled = true
| MCP:YQnf8p | jar.enabled = false
| MCP:YQnf8p | 
| MCP:YQnf8p | dependencies {
| MCP:YQnf8p |     implementation project(":core:core-enum")
| MCP:YQnf8p |     implementation project(":support:monitoring")
| MCP:YQnf8p |     implementation project(":support:logging")
| MCP:YQnf8p |     implementation project(":storage:db-core")
| MCP:YQnf8p |     implementation project(":clients:client-example")
| MCP:YQnf8p | 
| MCP:YQnf8p |     testImplementation project(":tests:api-docs")
| MCP:YQnf8p |     
| MCP:YQnf8p |     implementation 'org.springframework.boot:spring-boot-starter-webmvc'
| MCP:YQnf8p | }

data.content:
Based on my analysis of the repository, here's a summary of the module structure:

## Module Structure Overview

This is a **Spring Boot Java Template** designed for good initial development productivity with a modular architecture that can grow with the service.

### Major Module Groups

1. **Core Module Group** (`core/`)
   - **core:core-api**: The only executable module in the project. Responsible for providing APIs and setting up frameworks. Contains domain services organized to maximize initial productivity.
   - **core:core-enum**: Contains enums used by core-api that need to be shared with external modules.

2. **Clients Module Group** (`clients/`)
   - **clients:client-example**: Example module for HTTP communication with external systems using Spring Cloud OpenFeign.

3. **Storage Module Group** (`storage/`)
   - **storage:db-core**: Example module for database integration using Spring Data JPA with MySQL.

4. **Support Module Group** (`support/`)
   - **support:logging**: Provides logging support with distributed tracing (OpenTelemetry) and Sentry integration.
   - **support:monitoring**: Provides monitoring support with Spring Boot Actuator and Prometheus metrics.

5. **Tests Module Group** (`tests/`)
   - **tests:api-docs**: Convenience module for writing Spring REST Docs tests.

### Build Structure
- Uses Gradle with Spring Boot plugin management
- All subprojects inherit common dependencies: JUnit 5, AssertJ, Mockito
- Testing suites organized by type: unitTest, contextTest, restDocsTest, developTest
- Java toolchain configured with specific Java version
- Linting via Spring Java Format

### Dependency Relationships
- **core:core-api** depends on all other modules (core-enum, support:monitoring, support:logging, storage:db-core, clients:client-example)
- **storage:db-core** depends on core:core-enum
- Other modules have minimal dependencies focused on their specific responsibilities

### Source Code Structure (core-api)
The main application follows a layered architecture:
- **API Layer**: Controllers, request/response DTOs, config
- **Domain Layer**: Business logic services and domain objects
- **Support Layer**: Error handling, response wrappers
- **Configuration**: Async processing, exception handling

This template provides a clean starting point that separates concerns while keeping initial development productivity high, with clear paths for scaling as the service grows.
